article.title: Uber | SE2 | HLD
article.content:
Hello, 

I recently appeared for Uber SE2 interview. 
The question they asked in System design interview is kind of very different than standard HLD problems.

The interviewer asked me to design a dashboard for Restaurants with 3 major funtionalities.

1. Show number of order placed.
2. Show top 3 dishes for which order is placed.
3. Show total amount sell.


these data restaurant wants to see for last hr, last day, and last 7 days and in real time.

Assumption: 10k restaurants availabile and 100k order placed per sec.

I was not able to solve this problem in the interview :(

Can anyone please help me solving this?

post.content: KB -> Service -> Database and Service can send the events to Kafka or data streams and then flink can aggregate real time and store it in a searchable datastore. Shard based on restaurant round robin for scalability. Have multiple services, checkpointing the flink node in S3 for fault tolerance. Use Hyperlog to generate the top K dishes and cache them for reducing lead latency.

Whenever there is any question that is related to analytics across time, use time series database. In this case you can use something like druid or clickhouse or even a columnar db  to store the information. You can store it in multiple different ways
1. 1 table can contain the orders that were placed. Here you can have dimensions of resturant id, order id and total cost. Running a aggregate across this table will give you answer to number 1 and 3 along with filters on the restaurant
2. You can now either create another table or just add each dishes as another set of dimensions. Something like 
 `rest_id, order_id, cost, dish_id1, dish_id2`

The above will make a sparse matrix and you could get answer to all 3 of them. Note with above schema for number 2 you will have to run unique count on orderid. There are also some fancy approximations you can use to get that number. 

And to process the 100K events you need a streaming pipeline with pub/sub like kafka. 
You can practice more such questions here for free [https://hellopai.ai/.](https://hellopai.ai/.) The bot is tuned to ask followup questions based on your answers. Check it out!!

Restaurant

Menu
dish - cost

DB
dish - no of orders
totoal amount

There can be multiple restaurants

Phase 1
Solve it for one restaurants, then we scale
- Use Elasticsearch as the Database, where querying is flexible and fast 
- Maintain hourly data in the cache and write to Elasticserach at the end of hour.

Sorted Set (sorted on the count) Redis cache structure:
`key: global-sale
data {
	total amount
}`

`key: dish-id
data {
	price, count
}`

One each order:
- Update redis cache for dish-id, count++
- Update global sale, amount += price of the dish-id

At the end of hour
- Write the cached data to elastic search
	- hourly data can be retrieved directly from the Cache
- Summarize the elastic-search data into new index at the end of the day
	- The data for whole day (24 docs per dish) are summarized for the day. One doc per dish in a day.
	- this enables, queries for a day and week with less number of docs in the ES.

Phase 2
Solve it for multple restaurants, we just need to store and search data based on restaurant-id
REDIS
`key: global-sale + restaurant-id
data {
	total amount, restaurant-id
}`

`key: dish-id + restaurant-id
data {
	price, count, restaurant-id
}`
hourly, day and weekly queries to include restaurant-id

Components:
UI: 
- Where the oders will be recorded
- Shows hourly, daily and weekly top 3 sales.
  - UI can directly query ES for getting the top 3 sales for hr, day and week.

Kafka: 
- main service publishes order to Kafka after validation.
- main service consumes order one by one for further processing.

Main Service: 
- Orders from UI will come to the service through an API call.
- Validates oders, publishes to kafka. (minimal work, fast enough)
- Consumes from Kafka and processes orders (requires more compute)
	- each order is saved in REDIS maps with keys (global-sale+rest-id and dish-id+rest-id)

- Hourly Jobs to collect REDIS data, create document and publish to ES.
	- Quartz/Jobrunr jobs can do this work.
	- Read data from the REDIS cache.
	- Create doc and publish to ES using index `/date-hour-rest-id`

Summarization Service:
- 24hr jobs to summarize ES docs into new index `/date-rest-id`
	- Again use Quartz/Jobrunr Jobs.
	- Collect all docs from `/date-hour-rest-id` and summarize for the last 24hrs


Two microservices in total with Kafka and Redis. Deploy in AWS EKS which can scale as per load.
Thanks for sharing, does requirement 2 `Show top 3 dishes for which order is placed` means the Top dishes across ALL orders?\n\nAlso, which Location was this for? Thanks again!

We can divide the restaurant store in a n number of databases, based on location or create a cluster group to handle the load.
Based on traffic and order volume one database can serve single restaurant or m number of restaurant 
Above will solve the scale problem because, in a transaction database you don't want one restaurant order to effect other restaurant.

#1. To show order placement over time, instead of querying into relational database you can push the clientId, orderSuccess/orderFailure count in a timeSeries Database, which makes easy to pull the data
#2. Multiple approaches, pushing dishid and restaurantId in timeSeries db can do the job or running a aggregator every 5 mins which fetches the data from relational db for every resturant and put in cache or other way to use real time aggregator of with standard maxheap to caluclate the top 3 menu item
#3. This is per client so similar to #2, we can have aggregated query to do the job. We can async update the cache store with time hour, day or month key with the amount sell
